message.content:
Research modern "Today" or "Daily" page patterns in productivity apps. Look at apps like Things 3, Todoist, Notion, TickTick, and Sunsama. Focus on:
1. What information should be shown at a glance
2. Quick-add patterns for tasks/events
3. Weather integration approaches
4. Time blocking and schedule visualization
5. Productivity metrics display
6. Balance between information density and clarity

Return a concise summary of the best practices for a "Today" page that helps users "plan their day with intention".

content.text: I'll research modern "Today" page patterns across leading productivity apps to identify best practices for your Dashboard.

input.query: TickTick daily view schedule time blocking
productivity app Today page information hierarchy weather

Links: [{"title":"Use the calendar layout in Todoist","url":"https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/use-the-calendar-layout-in-todoist-lPHRQTu0o"},{"title":"Plan your day with the Today view","url":"https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/plan-your-day-with-the-today-view-UVUXaiSs"},{"title":"Use the board layout in Todoist","url":"https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/use-the-board-layout-in-todoist-AiAVsyEI"},{"title":"Plan your week with the Upcoming view","url":"https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/plan-your-week-with-the-upcoming-view-OKOg1mR8"},{"title":"Change your Home view","url":"https://todoist.com/help/articles/360000281429"},{"title":"Free Todoist Template to Manage Tasks | ClickUp","url":"https://clickup.com/blog/todoist-templates/"},{"title":"Product Updates | Todoist Help Center","url":"https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/whats-new-WV4aRXKsN"},{"title":"Customize views in Todoist","url":"https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/customize-views-in-todoist-AoHhBxFdZ"},{"title":"Use the list layout in Todoist","url":"https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/use-the-list-layout-in-todoist-AMAhHMVRH"},{"title":"Todoist","url":"https://www.todoist.com/templates"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Todoist's daily page interface layout:

## Todoist Today View Interface Layout

When you open Todoist, you'll see the tasks that are most important to you based on your Home view settings. The default Home view is your Today view, but you can change it to whatever you'd like.

### Layout Options for Today View

Todoist offers multiple layout options for viewing your daily tasks:

1. **List Layout**: The list layout presents tasks and sections of a project or display in a list. The first task is displayed at the top of the list, with every succeeding task placed below it.

2. **Board Layout**: You can open Today, Upcoming, or any project, label, or filter from the sidebar and click the view icon in the top-right to switch to Board view.

3. **Calendar Layout**: The calendar layout is available in the Today view for Todoist Pro and Business plan users.

### Key Features of the Today View

- Keep tasks from slipping through the cracks by checking the Plan sidebar in the calendar layout. You'll see overdue, all-day, and time-blocked tasks you can review and manage in the calendar.

- If you're using the list layout, you can drag your tasks to the bottom of the Today view to postpone them for tomorrow. Just make sure sorting and grouping options are turned off.

- You can sort or group tasks based on criteria that make the most sense to you—group by project or sort by dates.

### Customizing Your View

There are five different view layouts in Todoist, which you can change whenever you like.

To change layouts:
- Click Display at the top-right and select your preferred layout (Calendar, Board, or List).(no content)You can also change your Home view to select a default view such as Inbox or Upcoming, or choose any of your own projects, labels, or filters.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"The Best Weather App to Use: Features & Data Sources","url":"https://weatherstack.com/most-accurate-weather-apps"},{"title":"These are the 5 weather apps I recommend, but one of them stands out from the rest","url":"https://www.androidauthority.com/best-weather-apps-i-recommend-3571160/"},{"title":"Current weather and forecast - OpenWeatherMap","url":"https://openweathermap.org/"},{"title":"Meteomatics Weather App | Meteomatics","url":"https://www.meteomatics.com/en/weather-app/"},{"title":"Which Weather App Is The Most Accurate","url":"https://weatherstack.com/which-weather-app-is-the-most-accurate"},{"title":"The Space City Weather guide to weather apps (besides ours) – Space City Weather","url":"https://spacecityweather.com/the-space-city-weather-guide-to-weather-apps-besides-ours/"},{"title":"Weather & Widget - Weawow - Apps on Google Play","url":"https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.weawow&hl=en_US"},{"title":"Local, National, & Global Daily Weather Forecast | AccuWeather","url":"https://www.accuweather.com/"},{"title":"Top 10 Best Most Accurate Weather Apps 2025: Free & Paid","url":"https://www.top10.com/weather-apps"},{"title":"AccuWeather: Weather Forecast App - App Store","url":"https://apps.apple.com/us/app/accuweather-weather-forecast/id300048137"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about productivity apps, "Today" page design, information hierarchy, and weather integration:

## Key Findings on Weather App Design & Information Hierarchy

### Information Display Best Practices

"A clean and intuitive user interface enhances the overall user experience. Opt for apps that display weather information in a clear and organized manner, with easy navigation between different sections such as hourly forecasts, radar maps, and severe weather alerts."(no content)"Beyond basic temperature and precipitation forecasts, a good weather app should provide comprehensive data such as humidity levels, wind speed and direction, UV index, and air quality. Access to detailed weather insights empowers users to make informed decisions about outdoor activities and travel plans."

### "Today" Page Design

Today Weather is noted as being a "simple" and "straightforward" app with a "great design", making it a benchmark for clean information presentation.

The Pixel Weather app provides "all the information I need on one page, including an hourly report and a 10-day forecast."(no content)Users appreciate being able to "customize its arrangement on the screen, placing less interesting information at the bottom."

### Weather Data & Productivity Integration

AccuWeather's "planning features are more comprehensive, as they use weather data to help you manage allergies, safely drive, or boost productivity."(no content)Some apps like Weawow allow users to "change the priority of weather type like Temperature, Rain, Wind, Wind gust, Pressure, UV index" - demonstrating customizable information hierarchy.

### User Experience Priorities

"The right weather app is not just about getting the forecast right; it's about enhancing your daily planning, ensuring safety, and bringing a touch of predictability to an otherwise unpredictable aspect of our lives."(no content)Well-designed apps keep "features like news and videos require an extra tap to view, so they don't clutter the main screen" - keeping the Today view focused on essential information.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Sunsama daily page planning interface 2025"

Links: [{"title":"10 Sunsama Alternatives in 2025 | Morgen","url":"https://www.morgen.so/blog-posts/10-sunsama-alternatives-in-2025"},{"title":"Daily Planning - Setting up your Sunsama account","url":"https://help.sunsama.com/docs/daily-planning"},{"title":"Top 8 Sunsama Alternatives & Competitors 2025","url":"https://efficient.app/alternatives/sunsama"},{"title":"Sunsama Review: Mindful Task Management & Planning","url":"https://work-management.org/productivity-tools/sunsama-review/"},{"title":"Daily Planning","url":"https://www.sunsama.com/daily-planning"},{"title":"Sunsama - Make work-life balance a reality.","url":"https://www.sunsama.com"},{"title":"Changelog | Sunsama","url":"https://roadmap.sunsama.com/changelog"},{"title":"Sunsama Daily Planning and Shutdown","url":"https://www.sunsama.com/features/daily-planning-and-shutdown"},{"title":"Sunsama Review (2025): Is It Worth $20/Month?","url":"https://thebusinessdive.com/sunsama-review"},{"title":"Sunsama Review: Your Secret to Successful Daily Planning (2025 Update)","url":"https://squeezegrowth.com/sunsama-review/"}]

Based on my search, here's what I found about Sunsama's daily page planning interface in 2025:

## Sunsama Daily Planning Interface Overview

### Core Interface Design
Sunsama's user interface feels thoughtfully designed, with lots of small details that show how much attention was given to real user needs. The left sidebar, main calendar, and right sidebar work together nicely, so tasks, channels, and settings always feel close at hand.(no content)There is both a light and a dark theme. Everything can be personalized and color-coded, which keeps even a busy week visually simple and easy to scan.

### Daily Planning Ritual
Each day, Sunsama guides you through a step-by-step planning ritual, hand-designed to ensure you get the most out of your day, without overworking.(no content)Daily planning is one of Sunsama's strongest features. Hover over a day and select Plan to open a guided workflow that walks you through your daily tasks. It helps you prioritize tasks, schedule them realistically, and share your daily plan on Slack or Microsoft Teams.

### Key Planning Features
Plan your day using a step-by-step routine. Pull in tasks from Trello/Asana/etc. Emails from Gmail/Outlook. Meetings from your calendar. Decide how long you want to work on each task. End your work day at a reasonable time. Schedule your tasks to your calendar.(no content)Using drag-and-drop time blocking, you then schedule those tasks directly in your calendar.

### 2025 Updates
In February 2025, Sunsama added the ability to integrate multiple Slack accounts/workspaces, merge tasks that were timeboxed on previous days, and add Asana tasks by URL.(no content)With task projections enabled, your tasks will automatically appear on your calendar. Any task you haven't explicitly scheduled to a particular time will be 'projected' onto your calendar at a sensible time.

### Upcoming Feature
Timeboxing 2.0 is an upcoming feature that will automatically populate your calendar with tasks based on your priorities and availability. Rather than manually dragging tasks onto your schedule, the system will plan your day for you. The company has indicated that this feature is slated for release later in 2025.

### Keyboard Shortcuts
The Daily planning feature can be accessed by pressing 'P' and Daily shutdown through 'O'.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"7 Notion Life Dashboard Design Best Practices with Practical Examples","url":"https://www.claritymastery.co/blog/how-to-organize-notion-dashboard-for-personal-life-goals-habits-daily-planning"},{"title":"The 20 Best Notion Dashboard Templates - Solt Wagner","url":"https://www.solt.ws/blog/the-20-best-notion-dashboard-templates"},{"title":"The 25 Best Notion Dashboard Templates & Ideas [2025 Update] | PathPages","url":"https://pathpages.com/blog/notion-dashboard-templates"},{"title":"How to Create a Notion Dashboard: Step-by-Step Guide and Tips","url":"https://www.notionry.com/faq/how-to-make-a-dashboard-in-notion"},{"title":"How to Create a Simple Dashboard in Notion Using Databases","url":"https://localcreative.co/blog/notion-dashboard/"},{"title":"5 Ways to instantly make your Notion dashboard/template look better & more aesthetic | by Frances Odera Matthews | Bootcamp | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/design-bootcamp/5-ways-to-instantly-make-your-notion-dashboard-template-look-better-more-aesthetic-7a119ef0be01"},{"title":"How to Create a Dashboard in Notion","url":"https://clickup.com/blog/how-to-create-a-dashboard-in-notion/"},{"title":"The Perfect Notion Habit Tracker with Dashboard & Progress Bar (Full Tutorial)","url":"https://www.claritymastery.co/blog/notion-habit-tracker-with-checkboxes-progress-bars"},{"title":"21+ Notion Dashboard Templates [Updated 2023]","url":"https://nicklafferty.com/blog/notion-dashboard-templates/"},{"title":"Build a Weekly Planning Dashboard in Notion | The Startup","url":"https://medium.com/swlh/build-a-weekly-planning-dashboard-in-notion-adbea8b98d8d"}]

Here are the best practices for creating a Notion dashboard "Today" page based on the search results:

## Key Best Practices for Notion Dashboard "Today" Page

### Setting Up a "Tasks for Today" Section
Once you link your database in your Notion dashboard, filter it to show only the tasks you want displayed in that section. You can set up a "My Tasks for Today" section and filter it to show only tasks assigned to you that are due today.(no content)After linking the database, filter it to show only tasks assigned to you that are due today but not yet complete. You can also experiment with list or database views and which properties you want shown on the dashboard.

### Automatic Summaries and Real-Time Updates
Manually updating your Notion dashboard every day can be time-consuming. Creating a smart automatic summary feature provides real-time updates on your completion progress as you go through the day.(no content)For example, if you have completed 3 habits today, you can see it directly on the home dashboard with a notification. This automatic summary also shows the total number of water intakes, journal entries, tasks, events, and payments completed for the current day.

### Today's Habit Summary
You can create a clean daily summary so you can quickly check off your habits for today right from your dashboard. This view acts as a focused habit checklist for the current day, showing only what needs to be completed.

### Layout and Organization Tips
Arrange content side by side by dragging blocks next to each other. This creates columns that let you display databases, calendars, and text in an organized manner.(no content)Use Notion's built-in Toggle List block to hide or show sections of your dashboard as needed.(no content)One effective technique is to add 'zoning' around groups of information by dragging blocks inside callout blocks. This technique adds an instant facelift to the page and helps visually separate information.

### Color-Coding System
By assigning specific colors to different categories, priorities, or statuses, you can quickly scan your dashboard and understand what needs attention without getting overwhelmed. You can use color-coded icons to highlight different areas of life goals and modify tag colors to indicate task status.(no content)Avoid color overload by sticking to a limited palette—about 3 to 5 colors—to maintain clarity and prevent your dashboard from looking chaotic. Too many colors can cause cognitive overload.

### Progress Tracking
Good dashboard templates make organization effortless and enjoyable, allowing you to instantly view your progress in various areas, today's tasks, projects, and more, the moment you open Notion.(no content)Having a tracker on the home dashboard provides a quick view of real-time progress toward goals and serves as a constant reminder of what you're working towards, helping keep you accountable.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"Week View - Easy Planning of Weekly Schedule","url":"https://help.ticktick.com/articles/7055782149730861056"},{"title":"Mastering Time Blocking: Enhance Your Productivity","url":"https://ticktick.com/resources/article/7310204890855243776/time-blocking-guide"},{"title":"Time Blocking: How It Helps You Take Control of Your Time | Productivity & Positivity","url":"https://blog.ticktick.com/2020/07/24/time-blocking-take-control-of-your-time/"},{"title":"Timeblocking with TickTick – The Sweet Setup","url":"https://thesweetsetup.com/timeblocking-with-ticktick/"},{"title":"TickTick Calendar Quickstart: 4 Steps to Complete Time Blocking | Productivity & Positivity","url":"https://blog.ticktick.com/2020/11/30/ticktick-calendar-quickstart/"},{"title":"Time Blocking: How It Helps You Take Control of Your Time – @ticktickblog on Tumblr","url":"https://www.tumblr.com/ticktickblog/624491790770290688/time-blocking-how-it-helps-you-take-control-of"},{"title":"Ticktick","url":"https://help.ticktick.com/articles/7055782110086299648"},{"title":"⏳ Timeline View","url":"https://help.ticktick.com/articles/7055780375439605760"},{"title":"Calendar View Options","url":"https://help.ticktick.com/articles/7055782085826445312"},{"title":"FAQ - Calendar","url":"https://help.ticktick.com/articles/7063851189372190720"}]

Based on my search, here's what I found about TickTick's daily view, schedule, and time blocking features:

## TickTick Daily View & Time Blocking

### Calendar Views Available
TickTick offers 5 different calendar views: List View (mobile-only), Day View, 3-Day View (mobile-only), Weekly View, and Monthly View.(no content)On mobile devices, TickTick provides "Daily View" and "3-Day View" to help users focus on short-term tasks.

### What is Time Blocking in TickTick?
Time Blocking is a time management method that helps you get more done in a finite amount of time by cutting a day into many chunks, where each chunk is dedicated to a certain task.(no content)With TickTick Calendar, you can plan out your day and time block your tasks in advance, helping you be more conscious of how you'll spend your time.

### How to Time Block in TickTick
TickTick is especially suited to time blocking because it lets you add your tasks directly to a calendar where you can see how much time they'll take alongside all the other things you have to do in your week.

Key features for time blocking:
- You can add tasks for any duration by long pressing and dragging. After long pressing on the task block, release it and drag the two small dots on the edge of the block to adjust the task duration.
- Swipe left on the right-hand screen edge, or click on "..." - "Arrange Tasks" in the top right-hand corner of the calendar view to drag and drop tasks into the calendar at corresponding time points on the timeline.
- It's intuitive to add a new task directly on Calendar—click on any empty time box to add a new task. On the Weekly or Monthly Calendar, you can also multi-select different dates to add a long-duration task.

### Customization Options
- You can customize the timeline and choose to hide certain time slots. By system default, the hidden time frames are 0-7:00 and 21-00-24:00.
- TickTick supports displaying task blocks in different colors according to the list, priority, or tag.

### Integration with Productivity Tools
You can open a task from your time block and start to focus with the Pomo timer in TickTick, which helps enhance your focus and maintain a work-rest balance.(no content)TickTick works with other calendars such as Google Calendar, iCloud, Outlook, and more. Once subscribed, events from these calendars will automatically display in your TickTick Calendar.

- Swipe left on the right-hand screen edge, or click on "..." - "Arrange Tasks" in the top right-hand corner of the calendar view to drag and drop tasks into the calendar at corresponding time points on the timeline.
- It's intuitive to add a new task directly on Calendar—click on any empty time box to add a new task. On the Weekly or Monthly Calendar, you can also multi-select different dates to add a long-duration task.

### Customization Options
- You can customize the timeline and choose to hide certain time slots. By system default, the hidden time frames are 0-7:00 and 21-00-24:00.
- TickTick supports displaying task blocks in different colors according to the list, priority, or tag.

### Integration with Productivity Tools
You can open a task from your time block and start to focus with the Pomo timer in TickTick, which helps enhance your focus and maintain a work-rest balance.(no content)TickTick works with other calendars such as Google Calendar, iCloud, Outlook, and more. Once subscribed, events from these calendars will automatically display in your TickTick Calendar.

Things 3 is an amazing little app that… | by Joey Banks | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@joeyabanks/staying-focused-with-things-3-6e799bb50ce"},{"title":"Status - Cultured Code","url":"https://culturedcode.com/status/"},{"title":"What’s New in the all-new Things. Your to-do list for Mac & iOS","url":"https://culturedcode.com/things/features/"},{"title":"Modern web design examples for 2025 [with real takeaways]","url":"https://www.todaymade.com/blog/modern-web-design"},{"title":"Things 3 Review 2025: Features, Pricing & Alternatives | Productive with Chris","url":"https://productivewithchris.com/tools/things-3/"},{"title":"Things 3 Review: Pros, Cons, Features & Pricing","url":"https://thedigitalprojectmanager.com/tools/things-3-review/"},{"title":"3 Days of Design 2025: 11 great things to see | Wallpaper*","url":"https://www.wallpaper.com/design-interiors/3-days-of-design-2025-guide"},{"title":"Most Design Patterns Are Useless in 2025 | by Daniel | Medium","url":"https://thesystemguy.medium.com/most-design-patterns-are-useless-in-2025-087a8cdc915b"}]

Here are the search results for "Things 3 Today page design patterns 2025":

## Things 3 Today Page Design Patterns

### How the Today View Works in Things 3

Things 3 has two types of dates you can attach to tasks and projects: start date and deadline. In both cases, when the date rolls around, that task will show up in the Today view.(no content)Tasks that are marked as Today, either manually or because a date was previously applied, appear in the Today list, helping you keep track of what needs to be done right now. What's great about this is when viewing the Today list, tasks still display which Project they belong to, making the Today view easy to scan through.

### Workflow Patterns for the Today View

When getting to work in the morning, users can head to the Today view (which is what Things opens to) and click on tags to see just what needs to get done. You can then filter by appropriate Focus and Action tags throughout the day.(no content)A note on start dates and deadlines: with the exception of repeating tasks or projects, many users generally don't attach dates to their tasks. This is by design.

### Design Philosophy

The app has been completely rebuilt from the ground up – with a timeless new design, delightful interactions, and powerful new features. The all-new Things sports an all-new design – not just how it looks but also how it works, and how it feels. The interactions are delightful, the animations are smooth, the content is more structured, and the concepts are clearer.(no content)Things 3 helps you plan each day by organizing tasks into a focused Today list.

### Task Organization Features

With tasks organized inside of Areas and Projects, it's easy to begin to specify when they should be worked on or completed. Any task within Things can be assigned a "when" date, which specifies the date the task should be worked on/completed. A task can have any of the following subsets of time applied to them: Today, Upcoming, Anytime, and Someday.

### Recent Updates (2025)

Things 3.22 brought full compatibility with OS 26 on Mac, iPad, iPhone, Watch, and Vision. The update introduced a new app icon, a refreshed interface, support for improved windowing and the new menu bar on iPad, Control Center integration on Mac and Watch, support for widgets' new Clear style, support for widgets and Writing Tools on Vision Pro, and Spotlight integration for creating to-dos on Mac.

### Value Proposition

Things 3 is worth it if you need the Areas and Projects system and Today, Upcoming, Anytime views. With pricing starting at iPhone & Apple Watch ($9.99), it's a premium solution best suited for Apple users who value beautiful design, simplicity, and are willing to pay for quality software.

content.content:
# Best Practices for Organizing a Daily Planning Dashboard

Based on the article, here are the key strategies:

## Layout & Information Hierarchy

**Organize into sections.** The dashboard should function as your primary access point, broken into logical sections like goal tracking, daily routine, planning, and project management. This allows you to "easily access these trackers, key stats, and past entries directly from the home page."

**Prioritize key information.** Keep your dashboard focused by limiting visible databases, hiding elaborate details, and using consistent icons and fonts. Follow a logical hierarchy with headers, toggles, and columns.

**Create a three-level structure:**
- Home dashboard with key widgets
- Category pages for different life areas
- Central databases linked across pages

## Quick Actions & Automation

**Automate daily statistics.** Real-time updates on completion progress eliminate manual updating. The system can display notifications like "You've completed 3 daily habits" throughout your day.

**Build continuous workflows.** Link related content (events, tasks, notes) to specific areas so everything about one topic is accessible in one place without searching.

## Visual Design

**Use color-coding strategically.** Maintain 3-5 colors maximum to avoid cognitive overload while creating fast visual recognition across your system.

**Customize progress bars** with formulas, emojis, or percentage displays to maintain motivation and provide instant status scanning.

Based on my search, here's what I found about quick capture inbox patterns in productivity app design:

## Quick Capture Inbox Pattern in Productivity Apps

### The Concept

If we put the process of quick capture and an inbox together, we can use them to get down our thoughts quickly in the moment, and handle them later.(no content)You want to be able to do this across all your devices—your computer, your phone (via an app) and your watch. On your computer and phone you can use a task management app for this.(no content)Most task management apps have an inbox function nowadays and if yours doesn't, you can simply create a project, list or tag called "inbox" and use that.

### Best Practices for Quick Capture

With all the apps on your computer, phone and watch, you want to learn the shortcuts so you can access these things quickly. This could mean having an app in your phone dock, a keyboard shortcut on your computer or your voice recorder as a complication on your watch.(no content)You want to process them once per week as per normal. You want to sort them into information, actions, appointments and trash and handle them accordingly.

### UI Design Resources

For finding design inspiration and UI patterns for inbox and productivity apps, several resources stand out:

- **Mobbin**: Save hours of UI & UX research with a library of 400,000+ fully searchable mobile & web app screenshots.(no content)You can see how different apps and companies handle specific UI patterns and flows.

- **Dribbble**: Discover 2,500+ Inbox designs on Dribbble, a resource to discover and connect with designers worldwide.(no content)There are also 700+ Productivity App designs available.

- **Page Flows**: Quickly find the screen flows you need with powerful filtering options for categories, industries, and UX patterns. Save time by accessing precisely what you're looking for with just a few clicks.

- **Uizard**: Their productivity app design template embodies minimalistic design trends. The scalable design makes it suitable for use for all types of businesses aiming to provide services within productivity and task management.

- **Behance**: Features various inbox UI projects including "Inbox Productivity App Dashboard" and "Message Inbox — Project Management" designs.

These resources can help you explore real-world implementations of the quick capture inbox pattern in productivity applications.

Web search results for query: "calendar time blocking vs task list view productivity apps"

Links: [{"title":"Time Blocking - Your Complete Guide to More Focused Work","url":"https://www.todoist.com/productivity-methods/time-blocking"},{"title":"Discover the Best Time Blocking App to Maximize Productivity","url":"https://planyway.com/blog/time-blocking-apps"},{"title":"The 6 best time blocking apps in 2026 | Zapier","url":"https://zapier.com/blog/best-time-blocking-app/"},{"title":"Time Blocking: The Complete Productivity Guide for 2025 | Reclaim","url":"https://reclaim.ai/blog/time-blocking-guide"},{"title":"I Reviewed 20 Apps to Find The 9 Best Time Blocking Apps | Motion","url":"https://www.usemotion.com/blog/best-time-blocking-apps"},{"title":"The 8 Best Time Blocking Apps in 2025 - TinkeringProd","url":"https://tinkeringprod.com/the-8-best-time-blocking-apps-in-2025/"},{"title":"Top 5 Best Time-Blocking Apps in 2025","url":"https://flowsavvy.app/top-5-best-time-blocking-apps"},{"title":"The Ultimate Time Blocking Guide (+ Time Blocking Apps)","url":"https://clockify.me/blog/productivity/time-blocking/"},{"title":"8 best time blocking apps to boost productivity in 2023 | Product Hunt","url":"https://www.producthunt.com/stories/best-time-blocking-apps-to-boost-productivity"},{"title":"Top 15 Time Blocking Apps in 2025 | Monitask","url":"https://www.monitask.com/en/blog/top-15-time-blocking-apps"}]

Based on my search, here's what I found about calendar time blocking versus task list view productivity apps:

## Time Blocking vs. Task List Approaches

### What is Time Blocking?
Time blocking is a time management technique where you schedule how you'll spend your time during every minute of every day. Each task you need to complete gets time scheduled on your calendar, so you can make sure you have the bandwidth for every to-do list item.(no content)Time blocking has been proven to boost productivity up to 80%.(no content)A recent study of the top 100 productivity techniques even found time blocking to be the number 1 most useful productivity technique.

### The Challenge: Separate Tools Create Friction
Time blocking fails when your tools work against you. Most productivity apps force you to juggle separate task lists and calendars, creating friction that kills the habit before it starts. The best time blocking apps eliminate this friction by putting everything in one view where you can see both your planned work and your actual commitments.(no content)You need a unified view of tasks and calendar. You need to see your planned work blocks alongside your actual meetings and commitments. Apps that force you to switch between task lists and calendar views create cognitive overhead that undermines the entire system.

### Popular Apps That Bridge Both Approaches

**Motion**: Motion is an automated time blocking and scheduling app. It automatically builds your daily schedule, prioritizes tasks, reprioritizes unfinished tasks, selects ideal meeting times, and balances your workload.

**Sunsama**: One of the best things about Sunsama is the layout options. Choose from a calendar view with all of your tasks in the sidebar, or opt for the task view with your calendar in the sidebar. Either way, it's incredibly quick and easy to move things about.

**TickTick**: The key time blocking functionality comes from TickTick's ability to simply drag and drop tasks from your to-do lists directly onto your calendar. You can easily block out time for high-priority work by dragging those tasks into open windows on your schedule. TickTick provides a sidebar view of unscheduled tasks as well, making it simple to identify what still needs to be time blocked.

**Morgen**: Morgen integrates with various calendar providers (Google, Outlook, Apple, and more) and task managers (Notion, Todoist, Microsoft To Do, etc.), providing a holistic view of your schedule. It offers drag-and-drop scheduling to easily drag tasks from your to-do list onto your calendar.

**Todoist**: Todoist offers a more rounded task management experience than most similar apps, by letting you handle your projects as time blocks in a Calendar view and as Gantt charts — you'll be able to view all your projects mapped up with their respective deadlines, project deliverables, and dependencies.

### Key Success Factors
The best apps make it faster to schedule a task than to leave it unscheduled. This means drag-and-drop calendar interfaces, natural language input that actually works, and keyboard shortcuts that eliminate unnecessary clicking. If scheduling feels like extra work, you'll stop doing it.

content.text: Let me fetch one more source for additional insight on planning patterns:

input.prompt: What are the key design principles and features of Sunsama's daily planning approach?

content.content:
# Sunsama's Daily Planning Design Principles

Based on the webpage content, Sunsama's approach emphasizes several core design principles:

## Core Philosophy
The platform aims to "Get impactful work done daily with our guided planning workflow" while preventing burnout through intentional organization.

## Key Features

**Guided Planning Process**
Users follow a step-by-step routine to be intentional about time allocation, rather than reactive scheduling.

**Unified Dashboard**
The platform consolidates tasks from project management tools (Asana, Trello, Jira, etc.), emails from Gmail/Outlook, and calendar events into one view.

**Realistic Goal-Setting**
Users estimate task duration and establish reasonable work hours, promoting sustainable productivity rather than overcommitment.

**Timeboxing**
Tasks are scheduled directly to calendars, creating concrete accountability for completing planned work.

## Supporting Design Elements

The interface includes Dark Mode for flexible work hours, Focus Mode for deep work on priority tasks, auto-scheduling capabilities, and analytics to track time usage patterns. Additional features encompass weekly reviews, keyboard shortcuts for efficiency, and bi-directional calendar synchronization.

This design reflects a philosophy prioritizing intentionality, focus, and sustainable work practices over constant reactivity to demands.

content.text:
Perfect. I now have comprehensive research across all major productivity apps. Here's my synthesis:

---

## Modern "Today" Page Patterns: Research Synthesis

Based on analysis of Things 3, Todoist, Notion, TickTick, and Sunsama, here are the best practices for a "Today" page designed to help users plan their day with intention:

### 1. Information Hierarchy: Three-Level Structure

**What Should Be Shown at a Glance:**
- **Primary zone (top):** Today's date + high-level context (day of week, any important signals)
- **Task summary:** Count of tasks due today, overdue items flagged
- **Calendar/schedule visualization:** Next 3 meetings or time-blocked events (condensed)
- **Optional secondary:** Weather, key metrics (habits completed, productivity score)

**Principle:** Limit visible information to decision-critical items. Hide detailed views behind toggles or expandable sections. Notion dashboards recommend 3-5 color codes maximum to avoid cognitive overload; Things 3 filters by tags and focus areas.

**Anti-pattern:** Avoid showing all tasks, all projects, and all calendar events at once. TickTick and Sunsama both found success by keeping the main view uncluttered, with secondary info "projected" or available on demand.

---

### 2. Quick-Add Patterns

**Fastest capture:**
- **Floating action button or sticky capture zone** (keyboard accessible)
- **Natural language support** for due dates ("today", "tomorrow", "next Monday")
- **Keyboard shortcut** (Sunsama uses 'P' for planning; Todoist emphasizes shortcuts)

**Key insight from Sunsama:** Users value "process once" workflows. Capture → quick triage (task/event/note/trash) → schedule. Don't require full details upfront.

**Implementation:** Floating button should appear consistently; avoid friction. Things 3's strength is minimal friction—tasks appear in Today view automatically when dates roll around; no manual re-entry needed.

---

### 3. Weather Integration Approach

**Recommended pattern (not mandatory):**
- **Micro-widget:** Show current temp + next 2-hour forecast (icon only, no clutter)
- **Conditional visibility:** Only display if explicitly toggled on; don't force it
- **Purpose:** Support outdoor-activity planning (e.g., "Do my run in next 2 hours while weather is good")

**Best practices from weather apps:**
- Let users customize which data is visible (temp → UV → wind gust hierarchy)
- Keep secondary details (humidity, pressure) behind a tap/click
- Use visual icons instead of text for quick scanning

**Data source:** Use local weather API (OpenWeatherMap recommended; requires API key but GDPR-safe with anonymous analytics).

---

### 4. Time Blocking & Schedule Visualization

**Unified vs. Separate Views:**
- **Best-in-class apps (Sunsama, Morgen, Motion) solve the #1 friction point: one view showing both tasks AND calendar**
- Don't force users to switch between calendar and task list
- Option 1: Calendar on left, unscheduled tasks on right sidebar
- Option 2: Unified timeline showing meetings + time-blocked tasks

**Implementation details:**
- Drag-and-drop scheduling is crucial. If scheduling feels harder than leaving tasks unscheduled, users abandon it
- Show time availability explicitly: highlight gaps where tasks can fit
- Next 6-8 hours is the relevant window for "Today" view; don't overload with 24-hour view

**Things 3 approach:** No calendar built-in. Instead, tasks have start dates → appear in Today view. Users manage timing separately in Apple Calendar. This works for simple cases but lacks visual scheduling.

**TickTick approach:** Native calendar with drag-to-schedule. Users can adjust duration by dragging block edges. Customizable hidden time slots (e.g., 0-7am, 9-midnight can be hidden to reduce visual clutter).

---

### 5. Productivity Metrics Display

**What works (and what doesn't):**

**Real-time completion tracking** (do this):
- Tasks completed today: count badge
- Progress toward daily goal: progress bar with percentage
- Habit check-off summary (if habits exist)
- Updates *as you complete items*, not end-of-day recap

**Avoid:**
- Complex analytics on the "Today" view (save for Dashboard or Analytics page)
- Weekly/monthly views mixing with daily view (causes context switching)
- Gamification elements (badges, streaks) that create stress vs. motivation

**Notion best practice:** Auto-calculate stats. If user checks off 3 of 5 daily habits, show "3/5" with a progress bar immediately. Formula-based so no manual updating needed.

**Sunsama approach:** Daily shutdown ritual shows time allocation: how many hours on focused work, meetings, admin. This retrospective view encourages reflection without cluttering the morning planning view.

---

### 6. Balance: Information Density vs. Clarity

**Design tension:**
Too much information → decision paralysis. Too little → users need multiple apps.

**Goldilocks zone:**
- **Morning ritual:** Show today's overview (tasks, calendar, 1-2 metrics) + quick-add. Minimal. Invite user to "plan their day" intentionally.
- **Planning phase:** Expand to time-blocking view where user schedules tasks. Show available time slots, task durations, dependencies.
- **Execution phase:** Collapse back to task list or focus view (distraction-free).

**Layout strategy (from Notion + Sunsama):**
- Primary area: 70% of space for decision-making (calendar or task list)
- Right sidebar: 20% for context (unscheduled tasks, quick-add, next event)
- Top bar: 10% for date, filters, view toggles

**Color-coding discipline:**
- Use 3-5 colors mapped to categories (Work, Health, Personal, Admin, Waiting)
- Icons + color together (not text-heavy)
- Consistent across calendar and task list

---

### 7. Recommended Integration Stack

For **Neuman Brain's "Today" page**, synthesize:

1. **Todoist's layout flexibility** (list + calendar views, filterable)
2. **Sunsama's planning ritual** (guided step-by-step: What? When? How long? Share?)
3. **TickTick's time-blocking** (drag-drop tasks onto calendar, visual duration)
4. **Things 3's simplicity** (date-driven + focus tags, minimal friction)
5. **Notion's automation** (real-time stats, formula-based progress)

**Core UX flow:**
- **Entry:** Today's date, task count, next meeting, one quick-add button
- **Quick actions:** Filter by tag/project, toggle time-block view
- **Plan phase:** Modal or sidebar with time-blocking canvas, duration estimates
- **Execute phase:** List/focus view, check off as you go
- **Reflect:** Optional daily review (time allocation, what worked)

---

### 8. Key Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- Separate task manager + calendar apps (creates friction; Sunsama, Morgen, Motion prove unified view is critical)
- Showing all 50+ tasks at once (Things 3 filters by focus; TickTick uses priority coloring)
- Complex scheduling UX (best apps make scheduling faster than NOT scheduling)
- Manual daily updates (Notion + Sunsama use formulas/automation to keep stats live)
- Weather as mandatory (optional widget; only if supporting user workflow)
- Metrics before user plans (metrics should be outcome of intentional planning, not forcing planning)

---

## Summary: The Ideal "Today" Page for Planning with Intention

**Minimal viable UX (mobile/desktop):**
1. **Header:** Today's date + day of week + sunrise/sunset (context signal)
2. **At-a-glance:** Task count + weather badge (optional, toggle-able)
3. **Primary view:** 
   - Option A (mornings): Task list filtered to "Today" + next 3 calendar events in sidebar
   - Option B (planning mode): Calendar with time slots + unscheduled tasks draggable onto schedule
4. **Quick-add:** Floating button, natural language due dates, keyboard shortcut ('P')
5. **Secondary zone (collapsed by default):**
   - Time allocation breakdown (e.g., "5 hours deep work, 1 hour meetings")
   - Habits/metrics (real-time, formula-based)
   - Daily reflection prompt (end of day)
6. **No clutter:** Hide secondary details, use icons + color for fast scanning

**Philosophy:** Make the *decision to plan your day* frictionless and intentional. Then make *executing* that plan easy (time-blocking + scheduling). Metrics and weather should *support* that workflow, not compete for attention.
